Data Analysis and Data Wrangling skills when dealing with Huge Volume. 4. Performance analysis, troubleshooting and resolution including Cloudera Big Data eco system. 5. Experience with Linux-based infrastructures. Competencies: • Strong verbal and written communication skills; ability to face off to business users • Self-motivated individual and with determination to achieve goals • Willingness to learn, both technically and professionally • Strong analytical and problem solving skills • Good team working skills and ability to work in a distributed global team environment • Ability to work on a fast-pace environment; Flexible and able to deliver quality results in the required timeframe Roles & Responsibilities Development to analyze complex problems, evaluate business processes, system process ; and Industry standards ; and make evaluative judgements.

jobs.title: Preconstruction Analytics Engineer
jobs.description: Who We Are We're The Beck Group , the largest and most innovative integrated building firm in the country. We are focused on delivering design and construction excellence on a broad range of project types. Headquartered in downtown Dallas, with offices in Atlanta, Austin, Charlotte, Denver, Fort Lauderdale, Fort Worth, Mexico City, and Tampa, we believe a better process and outcome results from working together. At Beck, you will find yourself surrounded by passionate colleagues who have a collaborative mindset and care about each other. We are committed to hiring the best and brightest within our industry, and that's why we are seeking experienced professionals like you to help transform the industry and continue building Beck's respected reputation. What You Bring To The Table Beck is looking for a qualified Preconstruction Analytics Engineer to join an extraordinary project team. This role focuses on preconstruction analytics practices and subject matter expertise that supports the organization with reliable, timely, and fact-based historical project cost information. Employees in this specialty develop knowledge of historical cost data sources, data quality standards, data handling and preparation, and fundamentals of data modeling and reporting. The Position Involves The Following Essential Functions Gathering, preparing, and loading historical cost data from internal and external sources. Producing reports and insights from gathered data. Monitoring and assisting with estimate data quality, including meta data health checks, line item reviews, and coaching team members on proper data standards. Developing a foundational understanding of CMiC accounting system data (projects, job cost, subcontracts, vendors, departments, and changes). Gathering and reviewing economic data for escalation analysis. Updating the Beck Cost Index and assisting with quarterly projection updates. Producing escalation reports for specific date ranges and locations. Contributing to the bi-annual Beck Cost Report by preparing comparisons, updating workbooks, and incorporating internal feedback. Learning basics of DAX, Power Query, and data modeling for Power BI. Using Precon Analytics dashboards to provide insights or as tools for tasks. Taking DESTINI Estimator trainings. Supporting best practices and data quality in DESTINI Estimator. Supporting and developing functionality in Beck’s Excel Bid Tab. Who We Think Will Be a Great Fit A person with the willingness to learn and be mentored under talented Senior Preconstruction Managers. This individual has a comprehensive understanding of building construction, excellent time management and organizational skills, a passion to get things done, and possesses uncompromising authenticity and integrity. This individual must have interest in the integrated project delivery method, sharing physical and mental space with fellow design team members, and a deep desire for innovating in process improvement and technology. Physical Demands Frequently operates a computer and other office productivity equipment; frequently ascends/descends ladders and stairs; constantly works in various outdoor weather conditions; frequently moves equipment up to 50Ibs to various locations on site; constantly communicates with subcontractors, vendors, and other members of project team; ability to constantly move around job site on uneven surfaces; Spends much of the day standing. Ability to adhere to consistent and timely attendance. Beck’s Benefits At Beck our team member experience goes beyond your day-to-day work activities – we also want to support the rest of your life goals, milestones and challenges. We strive for thrive – we want you to thrive in your wellbeing, finances, and community. Benefits In addition to robust coverage for medical, dental, vision, life & disability benefits, Beck is pleased to offer its team members: 401k match and free SmartDollar program for financial wellness Free dedicated financial coach Personal health & fitness program for tracking activities & earning rewards Paid family leave Health discounts on medical premiums Free comprehensive health screenings Free health coach program for weight-loss & hypertension management Financial & behavioral support for family forming journeys (ex. Fertility, adoption, pregnancy, and parenthood). Free Life Coach Pet insurance discount Organized projects and events to support our communities Join our team and build your future with Beck. The Beck Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, disability, or genetic information. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. Nothing in this job description restricts management's right to assign or reassign duties and responsibilities to this job at any time.

jobs.title: Gen AI Engineer SME with Databricks
jobs.description: Role: Gen AI Engineer SME Location: Dallas, TX (Onsite) Duration: Long-term Contract Experience: 15+ Rate: $70/hr on C2C Job Description: GEN AI SME needed who can build Agents on Databricks that can help with the AI apps framework. In addition to that, Agent deployment, CI/CD experience will be good.

jobs.title: Data Engineer
jobs.description: Job Description We are seeking a Senior Data Engineer to play a critical role in transforming enterprise data capabilities within a large property and casualty insurance organization. This position is centered around building a modern, scalable, and business-aligned data platform to replace legacy, vendor-driven solutions tied to a recent Guidewire implementation. This engineer will help lead the design and implementation of a cloud-based data ecosystem leveraging Azure, Databricks, and Snowflake. The initiative is focused on bringing data ownership in-house, improving accessibility, ensuring audit readiness, and enabling enterprise-level reporting and analytics. You will partner closely with leadership, architects, and business stakeholders to define standards and influence the future of data engineering across the organization. This is a highly visible role with the opportunity to shape long-term data strategy, modernize pipelines, and establish best practices while mentoring a growing team. Key Initiatives & Impact Lead the design and development of a scalable Snowflake-based data platform to replace Guidewire DataHub (DHIC). Drive the transition from vendor-managed data solutions to internally owned, end-to-end data architecture. Build and modernize data ingestion pipelines using Azure and Databricks to support enterprise reporting needs. Establish data engineering standards, governance, and best practices to ensure data quality, integrity, and transparency. Enable structured, centralized access to data across multiple formats (e.g., Excel, PDFs, and other sources) for business users. Partner with cross-functional teams to define data architecture, storage structures, and data lifecycle strategies. Support enterprise-wide initiatives focused on data accessibility, audit readiness, and reporting efficiency. Influence technology decisions and recommend tools and frameworks to enhance the data ecosystem. Required Skills & Experience Strong hands-on experience with: SQL Azure (Data Services) Databricks Snowflake Proven experience building and scaling data platforms in cloud environments, specifically Snowflake. Ability to design and implement end-to-end data pipelines and data warehouse solutions. Experience defining data architecture, including data modeling, storage layers, and transformation strategies. Strong understanding of data governance, quality, and integrity best practices. Excellent communication skills with the ability to work directly with business stakeholders and leadership. Experience driving architectural decisions and recommending technology solutions. Ability to work with diverse data formats and structure them for analytics and reporting use cases. Master’s Degree required. Preferred Qualifications Experience in property & casualty insurance or broader insurance domain. Familiarity with Guidewire data environments and modernization efforts. Exposure to Kafka, Apache Spark, or similar distributed data processing tools. Experience collaborating with architects and contributing to high-level solution design. Work Environment Hybrid work environment (no regular onsite requirement; occasional in-person collaboration encouraged). 37.5-hour work week (Monday–Friday, 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM CST). Collaborative team structure with high visibility and leadership interaction. Opportunity to influence a growing data engineering function and mentor junior talent. Candidates must be authorized to work in the United States without current or future visa sponsorship. Why This Opportunity Stands Out Direct impact on building a next-generation enterprise data platform from the ground up. Opportunity to lead strategic data initiatives within a large, stable organization. High level of ownership and influence over tools, architecture, and engineering direction. Supportive environment with a focus on professional development and innovation. Job Type & Location This is a Contract position based out of South Sioux City, NE. Pay And Benefits The pay range for this position is $65.00 - $80.00/hr. Requirements Eligibility requirements apply to some benefits and may depend on your job classification and length of employment. Benefits are subject to change and may be subject to specific elections, plan, or program terms. If eligible, the benefits available for this temporary role may include the following: Medical, dental & vision Critical Illness, Accident, and Hospital 401(k) Retirement Plan – Pre-tax and Roth post-tax contributions available Life Insurance (Voluntary Life & AD&D for the employee and dependents) Short and long-term disability Health Spending Account (HSA) Transportation benefits Employee Assistance Program Time Off/Leave (PTO, Vacation or Sick Leave) Workplace Type This is a hybrid position in South Sioux City,NE. Application Deadline This position is anticipated to close on Jun 27, 2026. About TEKsystems We're partners in transformation. We help clients activate ideas and solutions to take advantage of a new world of opportunity. We are a team of 80,000 strong, working with over 6,000 clients, including 80% of the Fortune 500, across North America, Europe and Asia. As an industry leader in Full-Stack Technology Services, Talent Services, and real-world application, we work with progressive leaders to drive change. That's the power of true partnership. TEKsystems is an Allegis Group company. The company is an equal opportunity employer and will consider all applications without regards to race, sex, age, color, religion, national origin, veteran status, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information or any characteristic protected by law. About TEKsystems And TEKsystems Global Services We’re a leading provider of business and technology services. We accelerate business transformation for our customers. Our expertise in strategy, design, execution and operations unlocks business value through a range of solutions. We’re a team of 80,000 strong, working with over 6,000 customers, including 80% of the Fortune 500 across North America, Europe and Asia, who partner with us for our scale, full-stack capabilities and speed. We’re strategic thinkers, hands-on collaborators, helping customers capitalize on change and master the momentum of technology. We’re building tomorrow by delivering business outcomes and making positive impacts in our global communities. TEKsystems and TEKsystems Global Services are Allegis Group companies. Learn more at TEKsystems.com. The company is an equal opportunity employer and will consider all applications without regard to race, sex, age, color, religion, national origin, veteran status, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information or any characteristic protected by law. San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance: Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, for all positions located in the city and county of San Francisco, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records. Massachusetts Lie Detector: It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI): We may use Artificial Intelligence (AI) to support parts of our hiring process, including sourcing, screening, and evaluating candidates. AI helps assess applications and qualifications, but final decisions are made by our hiring team. By applying, you acknowledge and agree that your application may be reviewed using AI tools.

jobs.title: Data Engineer
jobs.description: CCaaS Data Engineer Location: Charlotte, NC / Sunrise, FL(Only locals) Visa-OPT only Experience : 6 Year sMust Have Skills#CCaaS #DataEngineer #ContactCenter #CCaaSEngineer #IVR #IVA #CTI #CRM #JavaDeveloper #SpringBoot #Microservices #Kafka #GCP #GoogleCloud #CloudEngineer #DataEngineering #AIML #MachineLearning #SARIMA #Prophet #DevOps #CICD #Jenkins #GitHub #Maven #SQL #TechJobs #CharlotteNC #SunriseF LCCaaS / CTI / CR MIVA / IV RJava, Spring Boot, Microservice sKafk aGC PCI/CD, Jenkin sMaven, Gi tSQL, RDBM SAI/ML (SARIMA, Prophet )Release Managemen tJob Summar ySeeking a CCaaS Data Engineer with experience in designing and implementing cloud-based contact center solutions, IVR/IVA platforms, Java microservices, Kafka, and AI/ML models. The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience with GCP, CRM/CTI integrations, CI/CD, cloud infrastructure, and data pipeline development .Responsibilitie sDesign and implement CCaaS, IVR, and IVA solutions .Develop Java/Spring Boot microservices and Kafka-based applications .Build and support cloud-native solutions on GCP .Integrate CRM, CTI, APIs, SIP/WebRTC, and contact center platforms .Develop and maintain data pipelines and datasets .Implement AI/ML models including SARIMA and Prophet .Manage CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins, Maven, and Git .Support Agile, DevOps, and production environments .Nice to Hav eModel lifecycle/MLOps experience .Knowledge of data structures, algorithms, and design patterns .Experience with TensorFlow, XGBoost, and Prompt Engineering .

jobs.title: Engineering Manager, Data Foundations
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>GitLab is the intelligent orchestration platform for DevSecOps. GitLab enables organizations to increase developer productivity, improve operational efficiency, reduce security and compliance risk, and accelerate digital transformation. More than 50 million registered users and more than 50% of the Fortune 100* trust GitLab to ship better, more secure software faster.</p> <p>The same principles built into our products are reflected in how our team works: we embrace AI as a core productivity multiplier, with all team members expected to incorporate AI into their daily workflows to drive efficiency, innovation, and impact. GitLab is where careers accelerate, innovation flourishes, and every voice is valued. Our high-performance culture is driven by our values&nbsp;and continuous knowledge exchange, enabling our team members to reach their full potential while collaborating with industry leaders to solve complex problems. <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OuZIb5zszQI">Co-create the future with us</a> as we build technology that transforms how the world develops software.</p> <p>*<em>Fortune 500® is a registered trademark of Fortune Media IP Limited, used under license. Claim based on GitLab data. Fortune 100 refers to the top 20% ranked companies in the 2025 Fortune 500 list, published in June 2025. Fortune and Fortune Media IP Limited are not affiliated with, and do not endorse products or services of GitLab.</em></p></div><h4><strong>An overview of this role</strong></h4> <p>As Engineering Manager at GitLab, you’ll manage and grow a high-performing engineering team within the Data Foundations group, working on a core data platform that ingests, processes, persists, and queries data streams generated across GitLab.</p> <p>We are looking for a leader who can leverage AI to drive non-linear productivity gains across the platform, accelerating our ability to deliver value to our customers.</p> <p>We’re looking for someone with deep distributed systems knowledge. You’ll need to be comfortable going well beyond people management and into the architecture of high-throughput, multi-component data systems: ingestion, buffering, enrichment, replication, storage, querying, backpressure handling, isolation, and production operations across multiple deployment models.</p> <p>You’ll partner closely with Product, Design, Infrastructure, Data, and other Engineering teams to evolve a platform that lives inside the product, keeps external services to a minimum, and runs across GitLab.com, Dedicated, Self-Managed, and Cells-based deployments.</p> <p>In addition to Data Insights Platform, this role will take on classic search scope as the team joins the Data Foundations organization. You’ll help lead architecture and execution across both GitLab’s analytics platform and classic search capabilities, balancing platform depth with customer-facing impact.</p> <p>You’ll help lead architecture and execution across both GitLab’s analytics platform and classic search capabilities, balancing platform depth with customer-facing impact.</p> <p>In this role, you’ll balance technical guidance with people management. You’ll hire, coach, and develop engineers while also helping drive architecture and execution across a platform built around stateless ingesters, Siphon CDC replication, NATS/JetStream buffering, enrichment pipelines, ClickHouse-backed storage, and a Query API that interfaces with the GitLab Rails monolith.</p> <p>In Data Foundations, we build the engineering systems that make platform data reliable, scalable, and available to product teams across GitLab, and you’ll help guide that work.</p> <h4><strong>What you’ll do</strong></h4> <ul> <li> <p>Hire, manage, and enable a high-performing Data Insights Platform engineering team, creating an environment where team members can do their best work and deliver strong results.</p> </li> <li> <p>Partner closely with product managers, product designers, and peer engineering managers to define and deliver the roadmap for <a href="https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/analytics-section/platform-insights/core/">Data Insights Platform</a> (DIP) and related Platform Insights initiatives such as <a href="https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/analytics-section/siphonhttps://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/analytics-section/siphon">Siphon</a>, Query API integrations, classic search initiatives, and self-service reporting foundations.</p> </li> <li> <p>Own delivery for your team, including planning, prioritization, execution, and operational follow-through across architecture work, platform improvements, and production readiness, with clear accountability for roadmap milestones and delivery outcomes.</p> </li> <li> <p>Guide the technical design of distributed data-path components, including ingestion, buffering, enrichment, exporting, and querying, and shape architecture choices on sharding, partitioning, component-specific scaling, failure recovery, and tenant isolation across SaaS, Dedicated, self-managed, and Cells deployments, with a strong focus on reliability, throughput, operability, and maintainability.</p> </li> <li> <p>Help the team design safe and scalable integrations with the GitLab monolith, including gRPC/Protobuf-based query paths and clear ownership boundaries between DIP and product teams building user-facing GraphQL or REST endpoints.</p> </li> <li> <p>Drive a high bar for security, privacy, and governance in how platform data is handled, including authentication, authorization, encryption, and safe handling of data with different privacy classifications.</p> </li> <li> <p>Improve operational maturity across the platform, including observability, metrics, logging, readiness, capacity planning, performance monitoring, and clear runbooks for managed environments, with a focus on improving availability, throughput, latency, and time to recovery.</p> </li> <li> <p>Collaborate asynchronously across teams and functions to help GitLab land customer-facing reporting capabilities on top of a strong, scalable data foundation.</p> </li> <li> <p>Lead the design and evolution of the platform with a focus on modular architecture, ensuring systems are extensible and ready for AI-driven integrations.</p> </li> </ul> <h4><strong>What you’ll bring</strong></h4> <ul> <li> <p>Experience managing platform, infrastructure, or data systems teams at scale, with a track record of building high-performing, values-aligned teams.</p> </li> <li> <p>Deep distributed systems expertise, including strong judgment on service boundaries, asynchronous pipelines, backpressure, fault tolerance, horizontal scalability, and operating multi-component systems in production.</p> </li> <li> <p>Strong technical background in backend and platform engineering, with the ability to guide architecture for high-throughput event pipelines and data systems.</p> </li> <li> <p>Experience with technologies and patterns relevant to this space, such as change data capture, event streaming or messaging systems, OLAP data stores, query-serving layers, and service-to-service APIs.</p> </li> <li> <p>Ability to hire, develop, and coach team members while still contributing technical guidance on complex architecture and delivery tradeoffs.</p> </li> <li> <p>Strong cross-functional collaboration skills, especially when ownership is split between platform teams and feature teams consuming common infrastructure or APIs.</p> </li> <li> <p>Experience building or operating systems across multiple deployment models, with sound judgment about the tradeoffs between GitLab.com, Dedicated, self-managed, and cell-based environments.</p> </li> <li> <p>Strong written communication skills and the ability to work effectively in an all-remote, asynchronous environment.</p> </li> <li> <p>Familiarity with search, indexing, and query-serving systems is a strong plus.</p> </li> <li> <p>A deep passion for reliability, customer outcomes, and engineering and operational excellence, with a proven track record of instilling these values in high-performing teams.</p> </li> </ul> <h4><strong>About the team</strong></h4> <p>In Data Foundations, we enable customers to self-serve reporting with scalable architecture. Our vision is to build a comprehensive dashboards-as-a-service framework that uses AI and relies on scalable data infrastructure.</p> <p>Data Insights Platform is one of our central initiatives, and we play a key role in advancing self-service reporting across GitLab. This is the foundational layer every reporting and intelligence surface at GitLab depends on — from product dashboards to Software Engineering Intelligence to the GitLab Knowledge Graph.</p> <p>In addition to Data Insights Platform, the team’s scope will include classic search as part of the broader Data Foundations organization.This brings together foundational analytics and search capabilities that help customers generate insights from product data and find relevant information across GitLab.</p> <p>We partner closely with product teams to turn DIP’s real-time insights, Postgres-to-ClickHouse replication, event routing, and downstream exports into dashboards, agent experiences, and customer-facing reporting.</p> <p>The scale expectations are real: we handle very large event volumes, with current and projected usage in the hundreds of millions of events per day.</p> <p>For more on how we work, see:</p> <ul> <li> <p><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/engineering/data-engineering/analytics/platform-insights/">Platform Insights Group</a></p> </li> <li> <p><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/engineering/architecture/design-documents/data_insights_platform/">Data Insights Platform architecture blueprint</a></p> </li> <li> <p><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/engineering/architecture/design-documents/data_insights_platform_querying_api/">Data Insights Platform Querying API</a></p> </li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>The base salary range for this role’s listed level is currently for residents of the&nbsp;United States only. This range is intended to reflect the role's base salary rate in locations throughout the US.&nbsp;Grade level and salary ranges are determined through interviews and a review of education, experience, knowledge, skills, abilities of the applicant, equity with other team members, alignment with market data,&nbsp;and geographic location.&nbsp;The base salary range does not include any bonuses, equity, or benefits. See more information on our <a class="waffle-rich-text-link" href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/" data-sheets-formula-bar-text-link="https://bit.ly/GLBeneUS" data-sheets-formula-bar-text-style="font-size:12px;color:#1155cc;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:underline;font-family:''Arial'';font-style:normal;text-decoration-skip-ink:none;">benefits</a><span data-sheets-formula-bar-text-style="font-size:12px;color:#000000;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:none;font-family:'Arial';font-style:normal;text-decoration-skip-ink:none;"> and </span><a class="waffle-rich-text-link" href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/stock-options/" data-sheets-formula-bar-text-link="https://bit.ly/GLequity" data-sheets-formula-bar-text-style="font-size:12px;color:#1155cc;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:underline;font-family:''Arial'';font-style:normal;text-decoration-skip-ink:none;">equity</a>. Sales roles are also eligible for incentive pay targeted at up to 100% of the offered base salary.</em></p></div><div class="title">United States Salary Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$152,800</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$259,200 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><h4><strong>How GitLab Supports Full-Time Employees</strong></h4> <ul> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/">Benefits to support your health, finances, and well-being</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/time-off-and-absence/time-off-types/#flexible-paid-time-off-pto">Flexible Paid Time Off&nbsp;</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/company/culture/inclusion/tmrg-tmag/">Team Member Resource Groups</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/stock-options/">Equity Compensation &amp; Employee Stock Purchase Plan</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/learning-and-development/growth-and-development/">Growth and Development Fund</a></li> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/#parental-leave">Parental Leave</a>&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>Please note that we welcome interest from candidates with varying levels of experience; many successful candidates do not meet every single requirement. Additionally, studies have shown that people from <a href="https://about.gitlab.com/company/culture/inclusion/#examples-of-select-underrepresented-groups">underrepresented groups</a> are less likely to apply to a job unless they meet every single qualification. If you're excited about this role, please apply and allow our recruiters to assess your application.</p> <hr> <p><strong>Country Hiring Guidelines: </strong>GitLab hires new team members in countries around the world. All of our roles are remote, however some roles may carry specific location-based eligibility requirements. Our Talent Acquisition team can help answer any questions about location after starting the recruiting process.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Privacy Policy: </strong>Please review our <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/hiring/candidate-faq/recruitment-privacy-policy/" target="_blank">Recruitment Privacy Policy.</a> Your privacy is important to us.</p> <p>GitLab is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. GitLab’s policies and practices relating to recruitment, employment, career development and advancement, promotion, and retirement are based solely on merit, regardless of race, color, religion, ance…

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description: SWBC is seeking a talented individual to work with Data Scientist, Data Engineers, and BI Developers to deliver data driven solutions that impact business performance, enhance business decisions, and support the overall data strategy of the organization. The Data Analyst will leverage AWS, Snowflake, Fivetran, DBT, and various BI Tools to manage, organize, and analyze data to provide business insights. The Data Analyst will also support various data initiatives to provide quick and accurate validation, strategy implementation, and address ad hoc requests from business partners. Why You’ll Love This Role As a Data Analyst in SWBC’s financial institution group, you will have the opportunity to collaborate with various data resources across the data pipeline to provide end-to-end data solutions, assist leadership by providing research, reports, or dashboards that promote data driven decisions across the organization. Bring your analytics research knowledge to work alongside various business units by providing insights, developing KPI’s, and establishing alert triggers to help optimize workflows. We follow Agile-Scrum methodologies. You will work with SWBC data program tools to understand how data flows from end-to-end, document business processes to create requirements for data model development and work with our data scientists to provide quick validation and tuning of machine learning models. Essential Duties Include The Following Develops and provides requirements for dynamic reporting solutions supporting both internal and external clients to include on time and accurate distribution of daily/weekly/monthly reports as required; and performs ad hoc reports and dashboard reporting options. Analyzes reports/dashboards for trends in activity and performance and provides data analysis results utilizing various data visuals such as charts, graphs, pivot tables, and the like. Utilize skills in quantitative analysis, data wrangling, and data storytelling to see beyond the numbers and understand how data can enhance end user decision making and process flow optimizations. Helps establish requirements and success criteria for Data Modeling and Data Engineering efforts to support the development of centralized data solutions for all end users and business units. Assists with the development and maintenance of documentation, governance, security best practices. Supports data validation, KPI development, data model calculations, dashboard development criteria, proof of concepts, and ad hoc reporting requests. Serious Candidates Will Possess The Minimum Qualifications Bachelor’s Degree in Data Science, Data Analytics, Business, Finance, Mathematics, Computer Science, related field, or equivalent experience. At least 1-2 years of experience in quantitative analysis in a fast-paced environment or across a diverse set of product lines. Experience in BI development and best practices across various BI tools (Tableau, Power BI, DOMO, or Looker Preferred). Experience in banking, payments processing, or risk management preferred. Experience communicating results of analyses to technical and non-technical leadership teams to influence the strategy. Strong understanding and ability to interpret ambiguity. Excellent and effective communication skills, both verbal and written. Proficient in various data specific coding languages (Python, SQL, Jinja, etc.) Able to make decisions and recommendations. Able to lift up to 20 lbs. of files, supplies, documents, or other related items. SWBC offers*: Competitive overall compensation package Work/Life balance Employee engagement activities and recognition awards Years of Service awards Career enhancement and growth opportunities Leadership Academy and Mentor Program Continuing education and career certifications Variety of healthcare coverage options Traditional and Roth 401(k) retirement plans Lucrative Wellness Program Based upon employee eligibility Additional Information SWBC is a Substance-Free Workplace and requires pre-employment drug testing. Please note, SWBC does not hire tobacco users as allowed by law. To learn more about SWBC, visit our website at www.SWBC.com. If interested, please click the appropriate apply button.

jobs.title: Pricing Data Analyst
jobs.description: Job Description Pricing Data Analyst San Antonio, TX Build an Aviation Career You’re Proud Of At StandardAero, we use our ingenuity and know-how to find solutions for the simple to the most complex challenges in aviation. Together, we get the job done and done well. Our stability, resources, and respectful culture supports you in building a solid career with a great team you can count on day in and day out for the long term. As a Pricing Data Analyst , you will act as the primary administrator of commercial program terms as it relates to credits, fees, and other contractual or commercial requirements across sectors within StandardAero. You will also work with Customer Programs, Finance, Supply Chain and any other related parties to generate, submit, and validate required reports and ensure that the required financial steps are followed internally. What You’ll Do Maintain pricing catalogs and maintain/validate relevant data to ensure accurate estimating, invoicing, and price book integrity Apply strong attention to detail to ensure accuracy, precision, compliance with contracts, and any additional requirements Ensure compliance, accuracy, and reliability through consistent processes and standard work Collect and validate required data for credits, warranties, and related adjustments, and route to Customer Account Leader for validation and approval Support program teams with data collection and preparation of customer cost estimates and invoices Partner with Finance to ensure transactions are accurately recorded, accounted for, and any accounting issues are resolved Submit reports in a timely manner and follow up to ensure payments are remitted and/or applied correctly Work with Finance/Credit and corporate functions to ensure payments are properly allocated to the correct sector/business unit Collaborate with Operations and Engineering to resolve data issues impacting estimates, invoices, credits, or royalties’ accuracy Perform data analysis, maintain datasets/databases, support administrative program tasks, monitor standard work for improvements, ensure SMS compliance reporting, track KPIs, drive continuous improvement, and complete additional assigned projects Basic Qualifications This position is open to new graduates, early career professionals, and experienced candidates. Post-secondary education or formal training in Finance, Accounting, Data Analytics, Business Administration, or a related field, or equivalent combination of education and experience. Proficiency in Microsoft Office, including Excel (e.g., data entry, sorting/filtering, and use of basic formulas such as SUM and AVERAGE). Demonstrated experience maintaining a high level of accuracy and attention to detail in data entry, analysis, or transactional work. Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to clearly document and communicate findings, issues, and recommendations. Strong organizational and time management skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines in a structured environment. Demonstrated analytical and problem-solving skills, including the ability to identify issues, evaluate options, and support data-driven decision-making. Self-motivated with demonstrated ability to learn new systems and processes and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives. Desired Skills Experience with business intelligence, reporting, or data visualization tools such as Power BI. Experience with SQL, ERP systems, and CRM platforms. Experience in the aerospace industry or a similarly regulated manufacturing environment preferred. Benefits That Make Life Better Comprehensive Healthcare 401(k) with 100% company match; up to 5% vested Paid Time Off starting on day one Bonus opportunities Health- & Dependent Care Flexible Spending Accounts Short- & Long-Term Disability Life & AD&D Insurance Learning & Training opportunities About Us Raising the Standard of Excellence since 1911 With over a century of proven excellence, StandardAero has become an industry leader in MRO services and customized solutions in the aerospace field. Our shared values and learning-based culture inspire our team to exceed their potential and power our customers’ missions worldwide. With on-the-job training, advancement opportunities, and excellent benefits, StandardAero invites you to experience a fulfilling and meaningful career with us. Inclusivity Is Our Standard It is StandardAero’s policy to provide equal employment opportunities to all qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including transgender status, sexual orientation, and pregnancy) sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, protected veteran or disabled status, or genetic information. Our supportive environment celebrates diversity with no room for harassment or discrimination of any kind. We invite you to bring your authentic self to our team and experience our welcoming culture.

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description: As a global medtech company, we are driven by our Vision of changing the trajectory of lives for a new day and our Mission to create ingenious solutions that ignite patient turnarounds. Our relentless commitment to patients and strong legacy of innovation in healthcare are the foundation of our future. If you're looking for a new chance, a new beginning, a new trajectory, LivaNova is where your talent can truly thrive. Join our talented team members worldwide to become a pioneer of tomorrow—because at LivaNova, we don’t just treat conditions — we aspire to alter the course of lives. The Data Analyst will provide data analysis, reporting, and visualization support to the Statistics & Data Science team across post-market surveillance, manufacturing, R&D, and clinical data sources. The Data Analyst will accomplish this by developing efficient, repeatable analytical solutions by automating routine reports, maintaining dashboards, and improving data workflows in collaboration with statisticians and business stakeholders. Additionally, they will translate stakeholder needs into practical data deliverables, with guidance, while ensuring outputs are accurate, validated, documented, and aligned with internal procedures. Essential Job Functions Generate routine and ad hoc descriptive analyses, summaries, tables, graphs, and reports to support operational, quality, product, and clinical decision-making. Develop, automate, and maintain recurring reports and analytical workflows using programming tools and reproducible code to improve efficiency, consistency, and timeliness. Build, enhance, and maintain dashboards and other data visualization tools that enable stakeholders to monitor key metrics and trends. Transform stakeholder questions into clear analytical requirements by clarifying objectives, defining deliverables, and confirming timelines, assumptions, and data needs. Partner with statisticians and subject matter experts to determine when a request is appropriate for a data analyst solution versus when deeper methodological or statistical expertise is required. Perform data extraction, data cleaning, data preparation, quality checks, and documentation activities to support reliable analyses and reporting. Support validation and code review activities for analytical programs and reports in accordance with internal procedures, documentation practices, and quality expectations. Identify opportunities to streamline existing reporting processes, standardize recurring deliverables, and implement practical process improvements that reduce manual effort. Communicate results clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders through effective written, visual, and verbal summaries, and manage multiple requests/projects with appropriate organization and follow-through. Minimum Requirements 0-3 years of professional experience in a comparable role supporting data analytics, preferably within a medical device, pharmaceutical company, or CRO. Bachelor’s degree with excellent track record in mathematics, statistics, data science, computer science, business analytics or equivalent (master’s preferable). Strong programming skills in R or Python, with experience in both. Advantageous to be familiar with SQL, Power BI, SAS, VBA, JavaScript. Understanding of mathematical and statistical principles. Willingness and ability to develop knowledge of device functionality. Ability to effectively collaborate and communicate cross-functionally with engineers and clinical or medical staff. Travel Requirements Occasional travel may be required. Pay Transparency A reasonable estimate of the annual base salary for this position is $70,000 - $100,000 plus discretionary annual bonus. Pay ranges may vary by location and will be awarded based on experience. Employee Benefits Include Health benefits – Medical, Dental, Vision Personal and Vacation Time Retirement & Savings Plan (401K) Employee Stock Purchase Plan Training & Education Assistance Bonus Referral Program Service Awards Employee Recognition Program Flexible Work Schedules Welcome to impact. Welcome to innovation. Welcome to your new life.
